tldr: Slint and Rectangle do not overlap — Slint is a GUI toolkit for building apps, Rectangle is a macOS window-snapping utility — so a migration only makes sense if you were misusing Slint for window management or are switching disciplines entirely.
why_switch: You only need macOS window snapping, not a UI toolkit
You want a free, zero-config tool with keyboard shortcuts and drag-to-snap
You prefer a turnkey app over building UI yourself
You're moving off cross-platform UI work and just need a productivity utility
You want zero telemetry and no account requirement
what_to_export: List of supported platforms you previously targeted
Custom widgets and components (kept as archive)
Any keyboard-shortcut conventions you want to mirror in Rectangle
Window-size presets your app used (for reference)
export_method: Slint apps live in your repo as plain source. There is nothing to 'export' — keep the project as-is or archive it. Consult Slint's official docs for any platform-specific build artifacts.
import_method: Rectangle is installed from rectangleapp.com or via Homebrew (brew install --cask rectangle) — confirm install method on the official site. On first launch, grant Accessibility permission and configure shortcuts in Preferences. There is no project import; configuration is per-user.

concept_mapping.note: App-level windowing in Slint vs OS-level window resize in Rectangle — not equivalent.
Rectangle ships built-in layout actions; consult official docs for the current set.
Configured in the Rectangle preferences pane.
Rectangle is macOS-only; no Windows or Linux equivalent.
Rectangle Pro adds custom grids — check official site for current pricing.

effort: 30 min - 2 hours (just install Rectangle and set shortcuts). Archiving the Slint project is bookkeeping.
gotchas: These products are not interchangeable — be sure Rectangle actually fits your need
Rectangle requires macOS Accessibility permission to function
Multi-monitor setups may need manual tweaks
No automation API for scripted layouts
If you needed full i3-style tiling, look at other tools — consult comparisons

email.subject: Switching from Slint to Rectangle
email.body:
Hi,

These two tools cover different problems, but for the use-case at hand (macOS window snapping) Rectangle is a better fit than building UI in Slint. I'll archive the Slint project and document the Rectangle shortcuts I land on. No migration tooling needed.

Thanks
